events.title: US and Iran Sign Deal to End Middle East War, Reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United States and Iran have signed a memorandum of understanding to end a nearly four-month war. A signing ceremony is scheduled for Friday, and shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz is expected to gradually increase. The deal, signed electronically by President Donald Trump, Vice President JD Vance, and Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf, aims to de-escalate regional tensions and restore maritime security.

events.title: Israeli Air Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 17
events.desc: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in the deaths of at least 17 people. Nine fatalities were reported in Tayr Debba following a series of attacks. This escalation occurs despite a fragile ceasefire and ongoing diplomatic efforts to secure a lasting peace.

events.title: Russian Strategic Bomber Crashes in Siberia
events.desc: A Russian Tu-22M3 strategic bomber crashed during a training flight in the Irkutsk region of Siberia. The aircraft's crew successfully ejected. The Tu-22M3, capable of carrying hypersonic missiles, has been a key component of Russia's strategic air power.

events.title: Drone Warfare Intensifies in Sudan, UN Reports Over 1,000 Civilian Deaths
events.desc: The United Nations reports that drone warfare has resulted in over 1,000 civilian deaths in Sudan during the first five months of 2026. The increasing use of drones signals a significant escalation in the conflict and a growing threat to civilian populations.

events.title: Four Palestinians Killed in Israeli Strikes in Gaza
events.desc: At least four Palestinians were killed in Israeli strikes and gunfire in Gaza. The incidents occurred as mediators engaged in further ceasefire talks in Cairo aimed at safeguarding a U.S.-brokered peace plan. One woman was killed in Zawayda and another person died in Nuseirat refugee camp.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Target Hezbollah in Beirut
events.desc: Israel claims to have targeted Hezbollah positions in Beirut. Iran has issued a warning that such actions could jeopardize the recently agreed-upon U.S.-Iran deal to end the broader conflict.

events.title: Iran and Israel Trade Direct Strikes
events.desc: Following increased fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, both Iran and Israel reportedly engaged in direct strikes on June 7. This exchange marks a significant escalation, potentially jeopardizing ongoing negotiations and regional stability.

events.title: Russian Ban on Cluster Munitions Violated in Mali
events.desc: Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets have been found in Mali, despite the country being a party to the Convention on Cluster Munitions. This discovery follows airstrikes announced by the Malian military, raising concerns about the use of banned weaponry.

events.title: Iranian Police Infrastructure Damaged in Attacks
events.desc: At least 80 police stations and infrastructure related to law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group have been damaged or destroyed in Iran amid the ongoing conflict. Experts suggest these attacks aim to degrade the regime's 'repressive capacity'.

events.title: UK Court Convicts Two Over Plot Linked to Russian Intelligence
events.desc: A UK court has convicted two individuals in connection with a plot targeting British government officials, reportedly orchestrated by a shadowy Russian figure. Reports suggest the attacks were part of a broader campaign of sabotage and misinformation attributed to the Russian state.

events.title: Amateur Saboteurs Used in Attacks for Gangs, Russia, and Iran
events.desc: Young individuals are being recruited as amateur saboteurs for attacks commissioned by gangs, Russia, and Iran. A recent case involved a Ukrainian national instructed to carry out arson attacks linked to a prominent British politician, highlighting the use of non-state actors in complex operations.

events.title: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Oil Tanker
events.desc: The U.S. military attacked a Palau-flagged oil tanker, resulting in the deaths of three Indian sailors. The U.S. stated the action was taken after the tanker failed to comply with directions. The incident raises concerns about maritime safety and potential miscalculations in the region.

events.title: Syrian Armed Forces Strike Targets in Homs Province
events.desc: Syrian armed forces reportedly conducted strikes targeting areas within Homs Province. The specific targets and extent of casualties remain unclear, but such operations are indicative of ongoing conflict dynamics in the region.

events.title: Zelenskyy Visits Damaged Kyiv Cathedral After Russian Strike
events.desc: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy visited a historic cathedral in Kyiv that was damaged by recent Russian missile and drone attacks. The visit highlights the continued impact of the conflict on Ukraine's cultural heritage and civilian infrastructure.

events.title: EU Fails to Agree on Sanctions for Israeli Minister Ben-Gvir
events.desc: European Union foreign ministers could not reach a consensus on imposing sanctions against Israeli far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir. The division within the EU highlights differing approaches to Israeli policies and the broader Middle East conflict.
